001package edu.cnm.deepdive.pippiphooray.controller;
002
003import android.app.DatePickerDialog;
004import android.app.Dialog;
005import android.os.Bundle;
006import android.widget.DatePicker;
007import androidx.annotation.NonNull;
008import androidx.fragment.app.DialogFragment;
009import java.util.Calendar;
010
011/**
012 * Dialog fragment that displays a date picker.
013 */
014public class DatePickerFragment extends DialogFragment
015    implements DatePickerDialog.OnDateSetListener {
016
017  /**
018   * Creates the date picker dialog using the current date as the default selection.
019   *
020   * @param savedInstanceState saved instance state, if any.
021   * @return configured date picker dialog.
022   */
023  @NonNull
024  @Override
025  public Dialog onCreateDialog(Bundle savedInstanceState) {
026    // Use the current date as the default date in the picker.
027    final Calendar c = Calendar.getInstance();
028    int year = c.get(Calendar.YEAR);
029    int month = c.get(Calendar.MONTH);
030    int day = c.get(Calendar.DAY_OF_MONTH);
031
032    // Create a new instance of DatePickerDialog and return it.
033    return new DatePickerDialog(requireContext(), this, year, month, day);
034  }
035
036  /**
037   * Receives the date selected by the user.
038   *
039   * @param view date picker view.
040   * @param year selected year.
041   * @param month selected month, zero-based.
042   * @param day selected day of month.
043   */
044  @Override
045  public void onDateSet(DatePicker view, int year, int month, int day) {
046    // Do something with the date the user picks.
047  }
048}
